name: Install and Manage Community Roles
description: Use Ansible Galaxy to discover, install, and manage community roles and collections to accelerate automation development.

steps.title: Search for roles on Galaxy
steps.description: Searches Ansible Galaxy for nginx roles that support Ubuntu.

steps.title: Install a role from Galaxy
steps.description: Downloads and installs the geerlingguy.docker role to the default roles path.
steps.tip: Jeff Geerling's roles are among the most popular and well-maintained on Galaxy.

steps.title: Install a collection
steps.description: Installs the community.general collection which provides hundreds of additional modules.

steps.title: Create a requirements file
steps.command:
cat > requirements.yml << 'EOF'
---
roles:
  - name: geerlingguy.docker
    version: "6.1.0"
  - name: geerlingguy.nginx

collections:
  - name: community.general
    version: ">=8.0.0"
  - name: amazon.aws
EOF
steps.description: Defines all role and collection dependencies with version constraints in a single file.

steps.title: Install all dependencies from the requirements file
steps.description: Installs all roles and collections defined in the requirements file.

fullScript:
# Ansible Galaxy workflow
ansible-galaxy role search nginx --platforms Ubuntu
ansible-galaxy role install geerlingguy.docker
ansible-galaxy collection install community.general
cat > requirements.yml << 'EOF'
---
roles:
  - name: geerlingguy.docker
    version: "6.1.0"
  - name: geerlingguy.nginx

collections:
  - name: community.general
    version: ">=8.0.0"
  - name: amazon.aws
EOF
ansible-galaxy install -r requirements.yml

faq.question: What is the difference between a role and a collection?
faq.answer: A role is a single unit of automation (tasks, handlers, templates). A collection is a larger bundle that can contain multiple roles, modules, plugins, and documentation. Collections are the modern distribution format.

faq.question: Where are Galaxy roles installed?
faq.answer: By default, roles install to ~/.ansible/roles. You can change this with --roles-path or by setting roles_path in ansible.cfg.
